mcqs.question: Which memory allocation technique suffers from external fragmentation?
mcqs.explanation: Both contiguous allocation and segmentation suffer from external fragmentation because free memory becomes scattered in non-contiguous blocks.

mcqs.question: In which technique is memory divided into fixed-size blocks?
mcqs.explanation: Paging divides memory into fixed-size blocks called pages (logical) and frames (physical), eliminating external fragmentation.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of virtual memory?

options.text: Allows execution of programs larger than physical memory

mcqs.explanation: Virtual memory allows programs larger than physical RAM to execute by using disk space as temporary storage, providing the illusion of larger memory.

mcqs.question: Which page replacement algorithm replaces the page that will not be used for the longest time in future?
mcqs.explanation: The Optimal algorithm (OPT) replaces the page that will not be used for the longest time in the future, achieving minimum page faults.

mcqs.question: Thrashing occurs when:
mcqs.explanation: Thrashing occurs when the OS spends most of its time swapping pages in and out of memory due to insufficient frames, causing performance degradation.

mcqs.question: In the FIFO page replacement algorithm, which page is replaced?

options.text: The first page brought into memory
The page with the smallest frame number

mcqs.explanation: FIFO replaces the page that has been in memory the longest, i.e., the first page that was loaded into memory.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?

options.text: To cache recent page table entries for fast lookup

mcqs.explanation: TLB is a hardware cache that stores recent virtual-to-physical address translations, reducing the time needed for address translation.

mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm is also known as the elevator algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: SCAN algorithm is called the elevator algorithm because it moves like an elevator, servicing requests in one direction then reversing.

mcqs.question: What type of fragmentation is caused by paging?
mcqs.explanation: Paging eliminates external fragmentation but causes internal fragmentation as pages may not be completely filled.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a non-preemptive disk scheduling algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: FCFS is the only non-preemptive algorithm among these; others (SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK) preempt when new requests arrive closer to the head.

mcqs.question: The working set of a process is:

options.text: The set of pages used by a process in a given time interval
The pages swapped out to disk

mcqs.explanation: The working set is the set of pages that a process has referenced during the most recent time interval (working set window).

mcqs.question: What happens when a page fault occurs?

options.text: The OS must bring the required page from secondary storage

mcqs.explanation: A page fault occurs when a referenced page is not in memory. The OS must locate the page on disk, load it into memory, and update the page table.

mcqs.question: In which memory management technique are processes divided into variable-sized segments?
mcqs.explanation: Segmentation divides a process into variable-sized logical units (segments) like code, data, stack, each with its own base and limit.

mcqs.question: What is swap space used for?

options.text: Holding pages temporarily when RAM is full

mcqs.explanation: Swap space is disk space used as virtual memory extension. When RAM is full, inactive pages are moved to swap space to free RAM.

mcqs.question: Which algorithm provides minimum page faults but is impractical to implement?
mcqs.explanation: The Optimal algorithm gives minimum page faults but requires future knowledge of page references, making it impractical.

mcqs.question: What is Belady's anomaly?

options.text: More memory causes more page faults in FIFO
Less memory causes fewer page faults
LRU has more page faults than FIFO

mcqs.explanation: Belady's anomaly is the phenomenon where increasing the number of page frames can cause more page faults in FIFO algorithm.

mcqs.question: The page table entry does NOT contain:
mcqs.explanation: Page table entries contain frame number, valid/invalid bit, protection bits, dirty bit, and reference bit but not process ID.

mcqs.question: In SSTF disk scheduling, the head moves to:

options.text: The next request in queue order
The closest request to current head position
The first request in terms of cylinder number

mcqs.explanation: SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) selects the request closest to the current head position to minimize seek time.

mcqs.question: What is demand paging?
mcqs.pages: pages are loaded into memory only when needed

options.text: All pages loaded at process start
Pages loaded only when referenced (on demand)

mcqs.explanation: Demand paging loads pages into memory only when a page fault occurs (i.e., when the page is first referenced), saving memory and I/O time.

mcqs.question: Which memory management scheme uses a page table and TLB?
mcqs.explanation: Paging (and segmentation-paging combination) uses page tables for address translation. TLB caches these translations for faster access.
